fields.definition_origin: Crystallized in 2023-2024 by Cal AI (Henry Langmack & Zach Yadegari, $30M ARR Year 1) — a 33-step quiz that builds a quantified user profile (height, weight, target, activity level, dietary preferences, blockers) and culminates in an LLM-generated macro plan reveal followed by a paywall. Predecessors: Noom (2018+), Flo (2017+), Lose It (2015+) — all health/wellness questionnaire patterns. Cal AI generalized it as 'data-collection-heavy sunk cost'. The taxonomy distinction from Mau Baron's story-onboarding (item 18) — Cal-AI style builds a METRIC PROFILE; Mau-Baron style builds an IDENTITY ARC. Same family (long onboarding), different mechanism.
fields.core_mechanism: Combines four levers stacked: (1) IKEA Effect (Norton et al. 2012) — 33 user-supplied data points produce a 'plan I helped build' that the user overvalues. (2) Sunk Cost Fallacy (Arkes & Blumer 1985) — 5-10 minutes invested make abandonment feel wasteful. (3) Endowed-progress (Kivetz 2006) — visible step counter compounds momentum. (4) Engineered Peak (Kahneman 1993) — the LLM-generated personalized plan reveal at step ~30 lands as the emotional high right before the paywall ask. The user is now most willing to convert because they have BOTH personal investment AND fresh demonstration of value.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Mesolimbic dopamine peak at AI plan reveal (variable reward + self-reference effect compound). Anterior cingulate cortex sunk-cost activity (Strough et al. 2008 fMRI of sunk-cost decisions). Ventromedial prefrontal cortex value-encoding of self-built plan. [uncertain on direct mobile-onboarding fMRI]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Cal AI: $30M ARR Year 1 (founder Henry Langmack interview, Mobbin / X teardowns 2024); 33-step flow documented in Mobbin captures and Cesar Alvarez's breakdown thread. (2) Flo period-tracker (Mobbin captures, TheAppFuel teardown 2024): height/weight/cycle/symptoms/health-conditions form a ~25-step quiz, $50M+ ARR (Sensor Tower 2024). (3) Puff Count smoking-cessation: 18-step pain survey then hard paywall, top-grossing health-cessation app 2024 (Sensor Tower). (4) Noom (2018+): ~32-step questionnaire still in service 2025; reported in TechCrunch as foundational to their $400M+ revenue (2022). (5) Adapty 2026 high-performing paywall report: long questionnaires correlate with the highest onboarding paywall conversion rate (1.35% topline) when paired with AI reveal. (6) RevenueCat 2025 State of Subs: AI-personalized onboarding apps see lower trial conversion (5.31%) but +14% direct purchase — questionnaire-built sunk cost shifts toward direct buy.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: End-to-end completion rate (Cal AI ~50% reach paywall is the high-water mark for 33-step), trial start at onboarding paywall (target 1-2% post-Adapty 2026 benchmark), Day-0 purchase rate, sunk-cost step (the step at which abandonment plummets — typically 60% of the way through), per-step abandonment curve, time-to-paywall (target 5-10 min for Cal-AI style).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Adapty 2026: onboarding paywall converts at 1.35% (highest of any placement) when paired with long questionnaire + reveal. Long-form vs short-form onboarding A/B tests in Mau Baron's playbook: 5→15 min extension tripled organic conversion (3× lift, but conflated with story changes). Direct lift attributable to questionnaire length specifically: unverified — varies wildly by category.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) State machine (XState v5) with 25-35 states, each rendering one question. (2) Shared `OnboardingContext` (item 14) accumulates every answer; persists to MMKV every transition; mirrors to server on each step for cross-device resume (item 33). (3) Chunked sub-progress (item 29) — section-of-section progress ('Section 2 of 4 / Step 2 of 8') prevents fatigue at step 18. (4) Mix question types (item 27): preference, demographic, goal, painpoint, identity, social-proof punctuation interleaved. (5) AI plan reveal endpoint (`/api/onboarding/plan`) takes the full context, calls LLM (GPT-4o or Claude), returns structured plan; pre-warm the call when user enters the loader screen. (6) Theatrical loader (item 13) for 4-7s while LLM completes; copy 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you...' (engineered, not a spinner). (7) Paywall directly after reveal — no intermediate screens. (8) Mid-onboarding review prompt (item 15) between reveal and paywall to capture peak. (9) Cross-step stitching (item 14) at every reveal moment to prove the 33 questions weren't wasted. (10) Per-step server post for fault-tolerance and analytics granularity.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI 33 steps: gender → birthdate → height → weight → goal weight → goal date → activity level → workout frequency → dietary restrictions → meal habits → ... → AI macro plan reveal at step 30 → review prompt → paywall. (2) Flo period-tracker ~25 steps: cycle regularity → cycle length → period length → symptoms checklist → health conditions → birth control → goals (track / TTC / pregnancy) → reveal → trial paywall. (3) Puff Count: smoking history → cigarettes/day → years smoking → top reason to quit → past attempts → health concerns → financial cost → personalized cessation plan reveal → hard paywall. (4) Noom ~32 steps: weight/height → goal → eating triggers → emotional eating Q's → sleep → social pressures → past diets → personalized weight-loss plan reveal → trial. (5) Calorie Mama: dietary preferences → allergies → goals → meal frequency → cooking habits → AI nutrition plan reveal → paywall. (6) Rise Sleep ~20 steps: sleep schedule → caffeine intake → screen time → goals → personalized sleep plan reveal → trial.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary. The 33-step flow is engineered for mobile thumb-zone, one-question-per-screen, swipe-forward UX. On web, equivalent flows (Noom web onboarding, Lose It web) get higher mid-flow drop because desktop users multitask and abandon — mobile's full-screen-attention is part of why the pattern works. Web equivalent: typeform-style one-question-per-page with progress bar.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) bolder left-aligned alert/onboarding typography is purpose-built for this pattern — single question per screen lands stronger. M3E (Android 16+) expressive shape system supports the chunked progress and themed reveal. Both DLs natively handle the slide transitions and translucent material backgrounds that make 33 screens feel kinetic rather than tedious.
fields.category_fit: Strongest fit: wellness/habit (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Puff Count, Headspace, Calm), AI-wrapper consumer (Cal AI, Calorie Mama, Plant Daddy with AI plan reveal), edtech (Headway, Blinkist), fitness (Strava onboarding 2024 redesign), journaling (Reflectly), period/fertility tracking. EXCLUSION: do not use for productivity B2B (Linear, Notion, Cron — value prop is universal, sunk-cost wastes admin time), fintech (KYC steps already long enough), social (premature investment), utilities (single-feature; use cinematic 4-screen item 25).
fields.tooling_options: XState v5, Plotline (visual flow builder, supports A/B variants, ~$99/mo), Adapty Pro (paywall + onboarding orchestration, ~$300/mo), Superwall (paywall A/B), Storyly (story-format onboarding), Onboardio (onboarding-as-service), OnboardJS, server-driven UI via Lovi protocol. AI plan: OpenAI GPT-4o-mini ($0.15/M input tok), Claude Haiku, Gemini Flash — for cost reasons use the smallest competent model.
fields.ai_dependency: required: the AI plan reveal at step ~30 is what converts the sunk cost into peak emotion. Without LLM personalization, the 33 questions feel extractive. Use a small model (GPT-4o-mini, Haiku, Gemini Flash) — quality requirements are modest because user inputs are structured and outputs are templated.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to model the full FSM with explicit step indices, accumulating context, theatrical loader, and reveal screen. Prompt fragment: 'Build a 33-step onboarding FSM in XState v5. States: q01_gender → q02_birthdate → ... → q30_loader → q31_reveal → q32_review → q33_paywall. Persist OnboardingContext to MMKV every transition. The q30_loader state runs for 4-7 seconds while the `/api/plan` endpoint generates a personalized plan; show theatrical copy ("Analyzing 2.4M users like you..."). The q31_reveal screen stitches three prior tokens into the headline. Forbid: skipping any step without explicit "Skip" confirm; loader < 3s; loader > 12s; reveal that doesn't reference user inputs.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the canonical Cal-AI-style flow in 8 representative screens (out of 33): (1) progress 1/33 "What's your goal?" 4 chip options. (2) progress 7/33 "How tall are you?" wheel picker. (3) progress 14/33 social-proof punctuation "Join 5M people losing weight" with avatar grid. (4) progress 22/33 "What's your biggest blocker?" 5 chip options. (5) progress 28/33 painpoint "How does your weight make you feel?" 4 emotion options. (6) progress 30/33 loader "Building your plan..." with animated rings + cycling copy. (7) progress 31/33 reveal "Here's how you'll lose 10 lb by July 4 even with a desk job" with macro card. (8) progress 33/33 paywall with side-by-side annual/monthly plans (post-2026-01 toggle ban). Use chunked sub-progress "Section 3 of 4" + "Step 22 of 33".'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) collapses 33 questions into one scrolling form (defeats sunk-cost), (2) shows a single spinner instead of the theatrical loader, (3) generates a generic plan reveal that doesn't reference user inputs (defeats stitching/IKEA), (4) places paywall before reveal (sequence inversion kills conversion), (5) skips the mid-onboarding review prompt (item 15 missed). Counter-prompt: 'Output 33 distinct screens as separate components. Each screen has ONE question. Sequence the loader (4-7s, NOT a spinner — must show step-cycling copy), then the reveal (must stitch ≥3 user inputs into headline), then the review prompt, then the paywall. Forbid: collapsing screens; generic spinner; reveal without stitched user data; paywall before reveal.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Collecting data without ever stitching it back (extractive feel — destroys trust). (2) 33 questions of identical type (homogeneous DMV-style — see item 27). (3) Reveal screen that's actually a paywall in disguise (no value shown). (4) Toggle paywall (banned 2026-01 Apple Guideline 3.1.2) — must use side-by-side plans. (5) Hidden cancellation behind 'progressive disclosure' (FTC Click-to-Cancel violation). (6) No skip / no back — 'No-Escape Room' antipattern. (7) Generic Celebration mismatched to archetype voice. (8) Asking demographic-heavy questions early ("What's your age range?") — feels like data collection not personalization; lead with preference / identity instead.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium-High. Long questionnaires often collect health/biometric data triggering GDPR Article 9 special-category requirements (explicit consent, special-category basis, DPIA may be required). Cal AI collects weight/health goals = sensitive. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) explicitly targets manipulative-consent UIs and addictive design — long sunk-cost flows could be flagged if paired with dark-pattern paywalls. FTC Click-to-Cancel (effective 2025-07-14) requires cancellation as easy as signup — a 33-step signup with 1-tap cancel violates symmetry. COPPA amended (2025-04-22, deadline 2026-04-22) — if mixed-audience, must use neutral birthdate gate (not 'Are you 13+?') and route under-13s to VPC. Disney $10M FTC settlement Dec 2025 demonstrates enforcement.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — must justify each question's necessity for core function. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (effective Jan 2026) bans toggle paywalls — long questionnaire MUST end in side-by-side plan selection or explicit timeline ("Today free / Day 7 reminder / Day 10 charge"). Android Play Data Safety form must declare every data type collected. App Store Review will reject if questions feel extractive without clear in-app value. ATT (App Tracking Transparency) prompt placement — fold into the soft-ask pattern (item 24), do NOT bury in the questionnaire.
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — 33 screens means VoiceOver/TalkBack users invest proportionally more time; provide screen-reader-friendly progress announcements ('Step 22 of 33, halfway there'). Each screen must have a single focused heading on transition. Dynamic-type / text-scaling — progress bar must scale with text. prefers-reduced-motion — replace slide transitions with crossfade; replace theatrical loader animation with static text. Skip / back must be keyboard-operable for external-keyboard iOS users. Long forms can fatigue users with cognitive disabilities; provide 'Save and finish later' (cross-device resume item 33).
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Latency: 33 server posts + LLM call = network-sensitive. Pre-fetch next screens; cache locally; offline mode shows queued state. (2) Question-quality decay — every PM wants to add a question, eventually flow becomes 50 steps with diminishing return. Lock at 25-35. (3) Reveal-quality is everything — if the LLM-generated plan feels generic, the entire pre-investment was wasted. Test the reveal extensively. (4) High abandonment in middle (steps 15-20) — engineered punctuation (social-proof screen, painpoint emotion screen) revives momentum. (5) Cost — $0.001-$0.01 per AI reveal at scale adds up; cache plans for similar input profiles. (6) Sandbox testing — Apple/Google IAP sandbox flow ≠ prod, paywall must be tested in real environment.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Pattern works globally but localizes heavily. LATAM: aggressive discount anchoring + monthly-equivalent pricing on the paywall (Adapty 2026 — localization 62.3% LTV win rate). JP/KR: testimonials and verifiable trust indicators in mid-flow social-proof screens; reduce 'pushy' copy. MENA: avoid Western body-image cues in weight-loss flows; ensure modest illustration. CN: super-app context — users tolerate denser screens but still abandon at length; consider 20-step variant. Translation alone insufficient — Adapty 2026 localization wins are not just language but pricing and copy register.
fields.prescribed_action: Use the Cal-AI long-questionnaire pattern ONLY for wellness/habit/AI-wrapper categories where personalization is load-bearing. Architect 25-35 steps as a state machine with one question per screen, mixed question types (preference / demographic / goal / painpoint / identity / social-proof punctuation), chunked sub-progress, an AI plan reveal at step N-2 with stitched user tokens, the system review prompt at step N-1, and a side-by-side paywall at step N. Never use for productivity B2B, utilities, or single-feature apps.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: This IS the questionnaire pattern — every question-design decision (sequencing item 26, type taxonomy item 27, conversational vs form item 28, progress indicators item 29) cascades from this pattern's choice.
fields.aha_relevance: The questionnaire's purpose is to BUILD UP TO the Aha — the AI plan reveal at step ~30 is the engineered Aha moment. Without the long pre-investment, the reveal lands as a stock template; with it, the reveal lands as 'the most personal product I've ever used in 5 minutes'.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Magician (transformation; AI reveal as the spell), Sage (wisdom from data), Caregiver (gentle pacing of difficult questions like painpoint). Mismatch: Outlaw (33-step compliance flow conflicts with rebellion), Jester (long flow kills playfulness — Mau Baron-style story 18 fits Jester better), Ruler (formal categories don't tolerate the soft sell at the end).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'Frictionless / Deferred Pattern' (item 19) — explicit opposite. Also pairs against 'Wasted Loading' since the theatrical loader (item 13) is engineered, not blank.
